content:
IVX EXPO GO — CURRENT DEVICE FAILURE REPAIR

CURRENT REAL DEVICE ERROR

java.io.IOException: Failed to download remote update

This proves Expo Go is NOT verified.

Do not return the previous tunnel report.
Do not say manifest 200 is sufficient.
Do not reuse the previous exp.direct URL.
Do not generate another URL until the phone-download path is verified.

==================================================
1. INVALIDATE THE FAILED SESSION
==================================================

Terminate:

- previous exp.direct tunnel
- previous Metro process
- stale Expo CLI process
- stale port 8081/8083 process
- old QR code/session

Return:

OLD_TUNNEL=
OLD_TUNNEL_STATUS=
OLD_METRO_PID=
TERMINATED=true/false

The owner must not reopen the previous URL.

==================================================
2. CAPTURE THE CURRENT FAILURE LOG
==================================================

Collect:

- Expo CLI log
- Metro log
- tunnel log
- manifest request
- bundle request
- asset requests
- phone connection attempt
- exact failure timestamp

Return:

PHONE_REQUEST_SEEN=true/false
MANIFEST_REQUEST_STATUS=
BUNDLE_REQUEST_STATUS=
FAILED_ASSET_URL=
FAILED_HTTP_STATUS=
TUNNEL_ERROR=
FIRST_REAL_FAILURE=

If the phone request never reaches Metro, classify this as tunnel/network
failure, not an IVX application crash.

==================================================
3. VERIFY EXPO GO AND SDK COMPATIBILITY
==================================================

Run:

npx expo-doctor
npx expo install --check

Return:

EXPO_GO_REQUIRED_VERSION=
PROJECT_EXPO_SDK=
REACT_NATIVE_VERSION=
EXPO_ROUTER_VERSION=
DEPENDENCY_ERRORS=
COMPATIBLE=true/false

Do not blindly upgrade the project.

If Expo Go must be updated, return:

OWNER ACTION:
Update Expo Go from Google Play.

==================================================
4. FIX EXPO-UPDATES CONFLICT
==================================================

Audit:

app.json
app.config.*
eas.json
android native update configuration
expo-updates configuration

Check:

updates.url
runtimeVersion
channel
branch
projectId
checkAutomatically
fallbackToCacheTimeout
requestHeaders

Expo Go development must load the current Metro development bundle and must
not be forced to download an incompatible production update.

Return:

UPDATES_URL=
RUNTIME_VERSION=
CHANNEL=
PROJECT_ID=
DEVELOPMENT_UPDATE_SOURCE=
CONFLICT_FOUND=
FIX_APPLIED=

==================================================
5. VERIFY EVERY REMOTE RESOURCE
==================================================

A manifest returning 200 is not sufficient.

Verify:

- manifest 200
- JavaScript bundle 200
- every required startup asset 200
- fonts 200
- splash image 200
- icons 200
- imported images 200
- no redirects to HTML pages
- no 401/403
- no 404
- no 502/503
- valid content types
- non-zero response sizes

Return:

MANIFEST_STATUS=
BUNDLE_STATUS=
ASSETS_TOTAL=
ASSETS_PASS=
ASSETS_FAILED=
FAILED_ASSET_URLS=
CONTENT_TYPE_ERRORS=

Expo documents that update failures can occur when required assets cannot be
downloaded, even when the update manifest itself is available. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

==================================================
6. REDUCE THE EXPO BUNDLE
==================================================

Current reported Expo bundle:

37.6 MB

This must be optimized before another device test.

Audit top 30 largest items:

- embedded images
- embedded videos
- JSON files
- admin modules
- owner modules
- recovery modules
- developer workspace
- AI modules
- duplicate packages
- source maps
- generated files
- unused screens
- eager route imports

Apply:

- lazy route loading
- dynamic imports
- defer Admin HQ
- defer Variables
- defer Developer Workspace
- defer Recovery Center
- defer owner-only AI modules
- remove accidental binary imports
- optimize image assets
- remove duplicate dependencies
- exclude temporary screenshots/logs
- exclude QA evidence files from bundle

Return:

BUNDLE_BEFORE=
BUNDLE_AFTER=
REDUCTION_PERCENT=
TOP_30_ITEMS=
TEMP_FILES_REMOVED=
OWNER_MODULES_DEFERRED=

Target:

BUNDLE_AFTER < 15 MB where technically possible.

==================================================
7. CREATE A CLEAN NEW SESSION
==================================================

Start from the correct IVX Expo directory:

npx expo start --clear --tunnel --port 8081

or Bun equivalent.

Requirements:

- one Metro process only
- one tunnel process only
- no stale cache
- no Watchman dependency required
- correct Git branch
- correct Git SHA
- correct Expo project

Return:

WORKING_DIRECTORY=
BRANCH=
GITHUB_SHA=
METRO_PID=
METRO_PORT=
NEW_TUNNEL_URL=
START_TIME=

The URL must be different from every previous failed URL.

==================================================
8. TEST THE NEW URL OUTSIDE EXPO GO
==================================================

Before asking the owner to open it:

- verify tunnel host resolves
- verify manifest from external network
- verify bundle from external network
- verify all startup assets externally
- keep tunnel alive for at least 10 minutes
- confirm no process restart
- confirm no tunnel disconnect

Return:

EXTERNAL_MANIFEST_STATUS=
EXTERNAL_BUNDLE_STATUS=
EXTERNAL_ASSETS_STATUS=
TUNNEL_ALIVE_10_MINUTES=
METRO_STABLE=

==================================================
9. OWNER PHONE CLEANUP
==================================================

Ask the owner to perform only:

1. Update Expo Go.
2. Open Expo Go Home.
3. Remove every old IVX recent project.
4. Android Settings → Apps → Expo Go.
5. Force Stop.
6. Storage → Clear Cache.
7. Do NOT clear app data unless cache clearing fails.
8. Disable VPN.
9. Disable Private DNS temporarily.
10. Disable ad blocker/firewall temporarily.
11. Confirm normal websites open.
12. Reopen Expo Go.
13. Scan only the newest QR code.

Do not reuse the old URL.

==================================================
10. LIVE DEVICE CONNECTION WATCH
==================================================

While the owner opens the new URL, watch logs in real time.

Return:

PHONE_CONNECTED_AT=
PHONE_IP_MASKED=
MANIFEST_DOWNLOADED=
BUNDLE_DOWNLOAD_STARTED=
BUNDLE_BYTES_SENT=
BUNDLE_DOWNLOAD_COMPLETED=
ASSETS_DOWNLOADED=
JS_RUNTIME_STARTED=

If the download fails, immediately return the exact failed request and status.

==================================================
11. DEVICE ACCEPTANCE
==================================================

Verify on the owner Android phone:

- IVX splash renders
- IVX Home renders
- bottom navigation renders
- Profile opens
- manual Owner Login opens
- no automatic owner login
- Admin HQ opens
- Access Control opens
- Members opens
- Investors opens
- Chat opens
- Owner AI responds
- reload works
- close/reopen works
- no blue Expo error
- no RedBox
- no fatal exception

Return screenshots from the actual phone.

==================================================
12. LAN FALLBACK
==================================================

If Tunnel still fails, test LAN only when:

- phone and computer are on same Wi-Fi
- no guest network
- no AP isolation
- no VPN
- valid computer IP is 192.168.x.x or 10.x.x.x
- Metro binds to 0.0.0.0

Run:

npx expo start --clear --lan --port 8081

Return:

COMPUTER_LAN_IP=
PHONE_LAN_IP=
SAME_SUBNET=
LAN_URL=
LAN_RESULT=

Never use a 169.254.x.x address.

==================================================
13. STOP RELYING ON EXPO GO IF TUNNEL REMAINS UNSTABLE
==================================================

If two clean tunnel attempts fail after:

- version compatibility passes
- manifest passes
- bundle passes
- assets pass
- phone network passes

then build a direct Android development APK.

Use EAS development build or local Android build.

Required result:

- installable APK
- no Expo Go required
- no temporary tunnel required for published update use
- IVX icon
- IVX package ID
- owner login
- production API connection

Expo documentation notes that development builds can load published updates
without requiring a particular local checkout or leaving a development
machine running. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}

Return:

APK_URL_OR_ARTIFACT=
BUILD_ID=
BUILD_SHA=
PACKAGE_ID=
INSTALL_RESULT=
LAUNCH_RESULT=

==================================================
FINAL VERDICT
==================================================

Return exactly one:

IVX EXPO GO VERIFIED ON OWNER PHONE

or

IVX EXPO GO FAILED — ANDROID DEVELOPMENT APK REQUIRED

Do not return VERIFIED unless:

- the newest URL opens on the owner phone
- remote update downloads completely
- all startup assets download
- IVX Home renders
- Owner Login renders
- Admin HQ opens
- reload and restart pass
- phone screenshots are provided
- no blue error remains

If failed, return only:

FAILED_LAYER=
FAILED_URL=
HTTP_STATUS=
FAILED_ASSET=
PHONE_REQUEST_REACHED_METRO=
EXPO_GO_VERSION=
EXPO_SDK=
BUNDLE_SIZE=
TUNNEL_STATUS=
ROOT_CAUSE=
APK_BUILD_STARTED=
NEXT_ACTION=
